frameworks.name: temporal difference reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: phasic and ramping dopamine encode reward prediction errors as TD-like signals

frameworks.name: value coding
frameworks.one_line: alternative account that ramping dopamine directly represents state value

frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: machine-learning framework providing the TD error formalism

core_question: Do slowly ramping dopamine signals represent state values or reward prediction errors, and can phasic and ramping dopamine be unified under one computation?
key_assumptions: midbrain dopamine activity reflects a single underlying computation across timescales
value functions evolve smoothly as the animal approaches reward
RPE and value can be experimentally dissociated using virtual-reality paradigms
manipulations: virtual-reality task designs that disambiguate RPE from value
measurement stage along the dopamine circuit (somatic spikes, axonal calcium, striatal dopamine)
dynamic stimulus signaling gradual approach to reward
key_findings: ramping dopamine signals are consistent with RPEs rather than value
ramping is observed at somatic spiking, axonal calcium, and striatal dopamine stages
a dynamic approach-to-reward stimulus is sufficient to drive ramping dopamine
interpretations: phasic and ramping dopamine are unified as a derivative-like computation over values on a moment-by-moment basis
slow dopamine ramps need not arise independently of somatic spiking
